Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Fisheries Act and other Acts in consequence
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to amend the Fisheries Act and other Acts in consequence
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Fisheries Act and other Acts in consequence

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled “An Act to amend the Fisheries Act and other Acts in consequence”.
This enactment amends the Fisheries Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: require that, when making a decision under that Act, the Minister shall consider any adverse effects that the decision may have on the rights of the Indigenous peoples of Canada recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, include provisions respecting the consideration and protection of Indigenous knowledge of the Indigenous peoples of Canada, and authorize the making of agreements with Indigenous governing bodies to further the purpose of the Fisheries Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: add a purpose clause and considerations for decision-making under that Act;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: empower the Minister to establish advisory panels and to set fees, including for the provision of regulatory processes;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide measures for the protection of fish and fish habitat with respect to works, undertakings or activities that may result in the death of fish or the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat, including in ecologically significant areas, as well as measures relating to the modernization of the regulatory framework such as authorization of projects, establishment of standards and codes of practice, creation of fish habitat banks by a proponent of a project and establishment of a public registry;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: empower the Governor in Council to make new regulations, including regulations respecting the rebuilding of fish stocks and importation of fish;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: empower the Minister to make regulations for the purposes of the conservation and protection of marine biodiversity;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: empower the Minister to make fisheries management orders prohibiting or limiting fishing for a period of 45 days to address a threat to the proper management and control of fisheries and the conservation and protection of fish;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: prohibit the fishing of a cetacean with the intent to take it into captivity, unless authorized by the Minister, including when the cetacean is injured, in distress or in need of care; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: update and strengthen enforcement powers, as well as establish an alternative measures agreements regime; and

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: provide for the implementation of various measures relating to the maintenance or rebuilding of fish stocks.
The enactment also makes consequential amendments to other Acts.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Subsection.Text: The definitions commercial, Indigenous and recreational in subsection 2(1) of the Fisheries Act are repealed.
The definition Aboriginal in subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Act is repealed.
The definition pêcherie in subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Act is repealed.
The definition analyst in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: analyst means a person who is designated under subsection 56.1(1) to perform the functions of an analyst; (analyste)

Subsection.Text: The definition fish habitat in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: fish habitat means water frequented by fish and any other areas on which fish depend directly or indirectly to carry out their life processes, including spawning grounds and nursery, rearing, food supply and migration areas; (habitat)

Subsection.Text: The definition fishery in subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: fishery with respect to any fish, includes,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any of its species, populations, assemblages and stocks, whether the fish is fished or not,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any place where fishing may be carried on,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any period during which fishing may be carried on,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any method of fishing used, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any type of fishing gear or equipment or fishing vessel used; (pêche)

Subsection.Text: The definitions autochtone and pêche in subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: autochtone Se dit, à l’égard de la pêche pratiquée par une organisation autochtone ou ses membres, de celle pratiquée à des fins de consommation personnelle, à des fins sociales ou cérémoniales ou à des fins prévues dans un accord sur des revendications territoriales conclu avec une organisation autochtone ou ses membres, conformément à la reconnaissance et à la confirmation des droits des peuples autochtones et des droits issus de traités au titre de l’article 35 de la Loi constitutionnelle de 1982 ou à des fins prévues dans toute mesure de mise en œuvre des droits convenue entre la Couronne et les peuples autochtones. (Indigenous)

pêche S’entend de l’action de capturer ou de tenter de capturer du poisson par tout moyen et, en outre, notamment des espèces, populations, assemblages et stocks de poissons pêchés ou non, du lieu ou de la période où il est permis de pêcher ou de la méthode ou des types d’engins, d’équipements ou de bateaux de pêche utilisés. (fishery et fishing)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Indigenous governing body means a council, government or other entity that is authorized to act on behalf of an Indigenous group, community or people that holds rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982; (corps dirigeant autochtone)
Indigenous peoples of Canada has the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982; (peuples autochtones du Canada)
laws includes the by-laws made by an Indigenous governing body; (lois)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Indigenous, in relation to a fishery, means that fish is harvested by an Indigenous organization or any of its members pursuant to the recognition and affirmation of Aboriginal and treaty rights in section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 or for any purposes set out in any rights implementation measure as agreed to by the Crown and Indigenous peoples; (autochtone)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: The heading “Purposes” after section 2 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after the heading “Purposes” after section 2:
The purpose of this Act is to provide a framework for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proper management and control of fisheries; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conservation and protection of fish and fish habitat, including by preventing pollution.

Subsection.Text: This Act applies in Canada, and also to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Canadian fisheries waters; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to a sedentary species, any portion of the continental shelf of Canada that is beyond the limits of Canadian fisheries waters.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (1)(b), sedentary species means any organism that, at the harvestable stage, either is immobile on or under the seabed or is unable to move except by remaining in constant physical contact with the seabed or subsoil.

Section.MarginalNote: Rights of Indigenous peoples of Canada
Section.Text: This Act is to be construed as upholding the rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, and not as abrogating or derogating from them.

When making a decision under this Act, the Minister shall consider any adverse effects that the decision may have on the rights of the Indigenous peoples of Canada recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.
Except as otherwise provided in this Act, when making a decision under this Act, the Minister may consider, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the application of a precautionary approach and an ecosystem approach;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the sustainability of fisheries;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: scientific information;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: Indigenous knowledge of the Indigenous peoples of Canada that has been provided to the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: community knowledge;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: cooperation with any government of a province, any Indigenous governing body and any body — including a co-management body — established under a land claims agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: social, economic and cultural factors in the management of fisheries;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the preservation or promotion of the independence of licence holders in commercial inshore fisheries; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the intersection of sex and gender with other identity factors.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, in order to carry out the purpose of this Act, establish advisory panels and provide for their membership, functions and operation.
Members of an advisory panel established under subsection (1) are to be paid any amount that is fixed by the Governor in Council for each day that they attend any of the advisory panel’s meetings.

The members are also entitled to be reimbursed, in accordance with Treasury Board directives, for the travel, living and other expenses incurred in connection with their work for the panel while absent from their ordinary place of work, in the case of full-time members, or from their ordinary place of residence, in the case of part-time members.
The portion of subsection 4.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Minister may enter into an agreement with any government of a province, any Indigenous governing body and any body — including a co-management body — established under a land claims agreement, to further the purpose of this Act, including an agreement with respect to one or more of the following:
Paragraph 4.1(2)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the circumstances and manner in which the government of the province or the Indigenous governing body is to provide information on the administration and enforcement of a provision of the laws of the province or the Indigenous governing body that the agreement provides is equivalent in effect to a provision of the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4.1(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsections (5) to (8), the Minister shall publish an agreement in the manner that he or she considers appropriate.

Before any agreement that is negotiated for the purposes of section 4.2 is entered into, the Minister shall publish the agreement, or give notice of its availability, in Part I of the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that he or she considers appropriate.
Within 60 days after the publication of an agreement or the giving of notice of its availability, any person may file comments with the Minister.

After the end of the 60-day period, the Minister shall publish in Part I of the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that he or she considers appropriate a report that summarizes how any comments were dealt with or a notice of the availability of that report.
The Minister shall publish any final agreement, or give notice of its availability, in Part I of the Canada Gazette and in any other manner that he or she considers appropriate.
An agreement entered into under subsection (1) must respect the rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.Text: Subsections 4.2(1) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an agreement entered into under section 4.1 provides that there is in force a provision of the laws of the province or the Indigenous governing body that is equivalent in effect to a provision of the regulations, the Governor in Council may, by order, declare that certain provisions of this Act or of the regulations do not apply in the province or the territory governed by the Indigenous governing body, as the case may be, with respect to the subject matter of the provision of the laws of the province or the Indigenous governing body.

Except with respect to Her Majesty in right of Canada, the provisions of this Act or of the regulations that are set out in the order do not apply within the province or the territory governed by the Indigenous governing body, as the case may be, with respect to the subject matter of the provision of the laws of the province or the Indigenous governing body.

The Governor in Council may revoke the order if the Governor in Council is satisfied that the provision of the laws of the province or the Indigenous governing body, as the case may be, is no longer equivalent in effect to the provision of the regulations or is not being adequately administered or enforced.

The Governor in Council may revoke the order only if the Minister has given notice of the proposed revocation to the government of the province or to the Indigenous governing body, as the case may be.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:
Every power that may be exercised in Canada by a fishery officer or fishery guardian under this Act may be exercised anywhere this Act applies.
Section 6 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 6.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In the management of fisheries, the Minister shall implement measures to maintain major fish stocks at or above the level necessary to promote the sustainability of the stock, taking into account the biology of the fish and the environmental conditions affecting the stock.

If the Minister is of the opinion that it is not feasible or appropriate, for cultural reasons or because of adverse socio-economic impacts, to implement the measures referred to in subsection (1), the Minister shall set a limit reference point and implement measures to maintain the fish stock above that point, taking into account the biology of the fish and the environmental conditions affecting the stock.

If the Minister sets a limit reference point in accordance with subsection (2), he or she shall publish the decision to do so, within a reasonable time and with reasons, on the Internet site of the Department of Fisheries and Oceans.

If a major fish stock has declined to or below its limit reference point, the Minister shall develop a plan to rebuild the stock above that point in the affected area, taking into account the biology of the fish and the environmental conditions affecting the stock, and implement it within the period provided for in the plan.

If the Minister is of the opinion that such a plan could result in adverse socio-economic or cultural impacts, the Minister may amend the plan or the implementation period in order to mitigate those impacts while minimizing further decline of the fish stock.

Subsection (1) does not apply if the affected fish stock is an endangered species or a threatened species under the Species at Risk Act or if the implementation of international management measures by Canada does not permit it.

If the Minister amends a plan in accordance with subsection (2) or decides not to make one in accordance with subsection (3), he or she shall publish the decision to do so, within a reasonable time and with reasons, on the Internet site of the Department of Fisheries and Oceans.

In the management of fisheries, if the Minister is of the opinion that the loss or degradation of the stock’s fish habitat has contributed to the stock’s decline, he or she shall take into account whether there are measures in place aimed at restoring that fish habitat.

Section.Text: The major fish stocks referred to in sections 6.1 and 6.2 are to be prescribed by regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Baux, permis et licences de pêche

Subsection.Text: En l’absence d’exclusivité du droit de pêche conférée par la loi, le ministre peut, à discrétion, délivrer des baux et permis de pêche ainsi que des licences d’exploitation de pêches — ou en permettre la délivrance —, indépendamment du lieu de l’exploitation ou de l’activité de pêche.
Subsection 7(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may refuse to issue a lease or licence for fisheries or fishing to a person, if, among other things, they are in default of payment of a fine in relation to a contravention of the Act and the proceeds of the fine belong to Her Majesty in Right of Canada or of a province or to any other person or entity.
Except as otherwise provided in this Act or regulations made under it, leases or licences for any term of more than nine years shall be issued only under the authority of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Sections 8 and 9 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Except if fees are prescribed in this Act, the Governor in Council may, by regulation and on the recommendation of the Minister, prescribe the fees that are to be charged for fishery or fishing licences and for fishing quotas.
For greater certainty, the regulations made under subsection (1) may provide for periodic adjustment of the fees referred to in that subsection.
Subject to subsection (2), the Minister may suspend or cancel any lease or licence issued under the authority of this Act, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: he or she determines that any provision of that lease or licence was not complied with;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: he or she determines that the lease or licence holder has, with respect to the lease or licence, entered into an agreement that contravenes any provision of this Act or of the regulations; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the lease or licence holder is in default of payment of a fine in relation to a contravention of this Act and the proceeds of the fine belong to Her Majesty in right of Canada or of a province or to any other person or entity.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may suspend or cancel a lease or licence under paragraph (1)(a) or (b) only if no proceedings under this Act have been commenced with respect to the non-compliance or contravention referred to in those paragraphs, as the case may be.

The Minister may, if he or she is of the opinion that prompt measures are required to address a threat to the proper management and control of fisheries and the conservation and protection of fish, make a fisheries management order with respect to any aspect of fisheries in any area of Canadian fisheries waters specified in the order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prohibiting fishing of one or more species, populations, assemblages or stocks of fish;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prohibiting any type of fishing gear or equipment or fishing vessel from being used;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: limiting the fishing of any specified size, weight or quantity of any species, populations, assemblages or stocks of fish; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: imposing any requirements with respect to fishing.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may impose any conditions that he or she considers appropriate in the order.
The fisheries management order may provide that it applies only to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a particular class of persons, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: persons who fish using a particular method or a particular type of gear or equipment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: persons who use fishing vessels of a particular class; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: holders of a particular class of licence.

Section.Text: Every person or holder to whom a fisheries management order applies shall comply with it.

Subsection.Text: A fisheries management order issued under section 9.1 shall be in effect for the term specified in the order, which shall not exceed 45 days from the day on which the order is issued.

If the Minister is of the opinion that prompt measures continue to be required to address the threat referred to in subsection 9.1(1), he or she may renew the order for a term that does not exceed 45 days from the day on which it is issued.

The Minister may amend a fisheries management order, other than its term, if he or she is of the opinion that the measures specified in the order are inadequate to address the threat referred to in subsection 9.1(1).
If the Minister is of the opinion that the measures specified in the order are no longer necessary to address the threat or if the threat no longer exists, he or she may revoke the order.

Notice of a fisheries management order shall be given to the persons or holders to whom it applies, in the prescribed manner or, if there is no prescribed manner, using a method provided under section 7 of the Fishery (General) Regulations, with any necessary modifications.

If notice is not given, then the contravention of a fisheries management order is not an offence under this Act unless, at the time of the contravention, reasonable steps had been taken to bring the substance of the order to the notice of the persons or holders to whom it applies.

Section.Text: If there is an inconsistency between a fisheries management order and any regulations made under this Act, orders issued under those regulations or conditions of any lease or licence issued under this Act, the fisheries management order prevails to the extent of the inconsistency.
Orders made under section 9.1 are not statutory instruments for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10:

Section.MarginalNote: Fees for services or use of facilities

Subsection.Text: The Minister may fix, by regulation, the fees to be paid for a service or the use of a facility provided under this Act by him or her, the Department of Fisheries and Oceans or any board or agency of the Government of Canada for which he or she has responsibility.
Fees that are fixed under subsection (1) shall not exceed the cost to Her Majesty in right of Canada of providing the service or the use of the facility.

Section.MarginalNote: Fees for products or cost recovery

Subsection.Text: The Minister may fix, by regulation, fees in respect of the products provided or the recovery, in whole or in part, of costs that are incurred in relation to the administration of this Act by him or her, the Department of Fisheries and Oceans or any board or agency of the Government of Canada for which he or she has responsibility.
Fees in respect of the product or recovery of costs that are fixed under subsection (1) shall not exceed the cost to Her Majesty in right of Canada of providing the product or recovering of costs.

Section.Text: Subject to section 8, the Minister may fix, by regulation, fees payable in respect of a conferral, by means of a permit or authorization, of a right or privilege provided under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may fix, by regulation, fees in respect of the provision of regulatory processes under this Act by him or her, the Department of Fisheries and Oceans or any board or agency of the Government of Canada for which he or she has responsibility.

Fees that are fixed under subsection (1) shall in the aggregate not exceed an amount sufficient to compensate Her Majesty in right of Canada for any reasonable expenses incurred by Her Majesty for the purpose of providing regulatory processes under this Act.

Section.Text: Regulations made under any of sections 11 to 14 may provide for the periodic adjustment of the fees referred to in those sections.
Any fees charged in relation to the issuance of a licence by an employee of a provincial government belong to Her Majesty in right of that province.
Section 20 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Prevention of the Escape of Fish

Section.Text: Section 23 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Défense de pêcher dans les zones louées à d’autres
Section.Text: Il est interdit de pêcher ou de tuer du poisson dans les eaux, sur la grève ou dans une pêche mentionnées dans un bail ou une licence, ou d’y mouiller ou utiliser quelque engin ou appareil de pêche, sans la permission de l’occupant selon le bail ou la licence alors en vigueur; il est également interdit de troubler ou d’endommager pareille pêche.
Section 24 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), no one shall fish for a cetacean with the intent to take it into captivity.

The Minister may, subject to any conditions that he or she may specify, authorize a person to fish for a cetacean with the intent to take it into captivity if he or she is of the opinion that the circumstances so require, including when the cetacean is injured or in distress or is in need of care.

No person shall import into Canada or export from Canada, or attempt to import or export, a living cetacean or sperm, an egg or an embryo of a cetacean, except in accordance with a permit issued under subsection (2).

The Minister may issue a permit authorizing the importation or exportation of a living cetacean or sperm, an egg or an embryo of a cetacean and impose any conditions that the Minister considers appropriate in the permit, if the importation or exportation is for the purpose of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: conducting scientific research; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: keeping the cetacean in captivity if it is in the best interests of the cetacean’s welfare to do so.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel a permit issued under subsection (2).

Section.MarginalNote: Seines, nets, etc., not to obstruct navigation
Section.Text: Seines, nets or other fishing apparatus shall not be set or used in such a manner or in such a place that they or any equipment that is attached to any of them obstructs the navigation of boats and vessels and no boats or vessels shall destroy or wantonly injure in any way seines, nets or other fishing apparatus lawfully set or used or any equipment that is attached to any of them.
Subsections 25(1) and (2) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Installation d’engins de pêche en période d’interdiction

Subsection.Text: Sous réserve des règlements, il est interdit de placer des engins ou appareils de pêche dans les eaux, sur la grève ou dans une pêche durant une période d’interdiction.

Sous réserve des règlements et du paragraphe (3), les personnes qui placent des engins ou appareils de pêche dans les eaux, sur la grève ou dans une pêche sont tenues de les enlever dès qu’elles ont cessé de s’en servir et au plus tard avant le début de la période d’interdiction.

Section.Text: Section 28 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 29(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Obstructing passage of fish or waters

Subsection.Text: No person shall, for the purpose of fishing, place, erect, use or maintain any seine, net, weir or other fishing gear or apparatus, or any log, rock or material of any kind that
Subsection 29(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister or a fishery officer may order the removal of or remove any seine, net, weir or other fishing gear or apparatus, or any log, rock or material of any kind that, in the opinion of the Minister or fishery officer, results in an obstruction referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (b).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 31:

Subsection.Text: No person shall engage in the practice of shark finning.
In this section, shark finning means the practice of removing the fins from a shark and discarding the remainder of the shark while at sea.

No person shall import into Canada or export from Canada, or attempt to so import or export, any shark fins or parts of shark fins that are not attached to a shark carcass except in accordance with a permit issued under subsection (2).

The Minister may issue a permit authorizing the importation or exportation into or from Canada of any shark fins or parts of shark fins that are not attached to a shark carcass and impose any conditions that the Minister considers appropriate in the permit

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the importation or exportation is for the purpose of scientific research relating to shark conservation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if, in the Minister’s opinion, the scientific research is likely to benefit the survival of any shark species or is required to enhance the chances of survival of any shark species in the wild.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel a permit issued under subsection (2).

Section.Text: The heading before section 34 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Fish and Fish Habitat Protection and Pollution Prevention

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 34(1) of the Act before the first definition is replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this section and sections 34.1 to 42.5.
Subsection 34(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: designated project means a project that is designated by regulations made under paragraph 43(1)(i.5) or that belongs to a class of projects that is designated by those regulations and that consists of works, undertakings or activities, including any works, undertakings or activities that the Minister designates to be associated with the project; (projet désigné)
ecologically significant area means an area designated by regulations made under subsection 35.2(2); (zone d’importance écologique)

Subsection.Text: Section 34 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Any provision of this Act that applies to works, undertakings or activities also applies to the works, undertakings or activities of a designated project, except paragraphs 34.4(2)(a) to (c) and (e) and 35(2)(a) to (c) and (e).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 34:

Subsection.Text: Before recommending to the Governor in Council that a regulation be made in respect of section 34.4, 35 or 35.1 or under subsection 35.2(10), 36(5) or (5.1), paragraph 43(1)(b.2) or subsection 43(5) or before exercising any power under subsection 34.3(2), (3) or (7), paragraph 34.4(2)(b) or (c), subsection 34.4(4), paragraph 35(2)(b) or (c) or subsection 35(4), 35.1(3), 35.2(7) or 36(5.2), or under subsection 37(2) with regard to an offence under subsection 40(1), the Minister, prescribed person or prescribed entity, as the case may be, shall consider the following factors:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contribution to the productivity of relevant fisheries by the fish or fish habitat that is likely to be affected;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fisheries management objectives;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether there are measures and standards

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to avoid the death of fish or to mitigate the extent of their death or offset their death, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to avoid, mitigate or offset the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the cumulative effects of the carrying on of the work, undertaking or activity referred to in a recommendation or an exercise of power, in combination with other works, undertakings or activities that have been or are being carried on, on fish and fish habitat;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any fish habitat banks, as defined in section 42.01, that may be affected;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: whether any measures and standards to offset the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat give priority to the restoration of degraded fish habitat;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: Indigenous knowledge of the Indigenous peoples of Canada that has been provided to the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: any other factor that the Minister considers relevant.

Subsection.Text: The obligation to consider the factors set out in subsection (1) applies only to the recommendations and powers that continue to be made or exercised by the Minister after an order is made under subsection 43.2(1) that sets out the powers, duties or functions that the designated minister may exercise or perform.
The Minister may establish standards and codes of practice for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the avoidance of death to fish and harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conservation and protection of fish or fish habitat; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the prevention of pollution.

Subsection.Text: The standards and codes of practice may specify procedures, practices or standards in relation to works, undertakings and activities during any phase of their construction, operation, modification, decommissioning or abandonment.

Before establishing any standards and codes of practice, the Minister may consult with any provincial government, any Indigenous governing body, any government department or agency or any persons interested in the protection of fish or fish habitat and the prevention of pollution.

The Minister shall publish any standards and codes of practice established under this section, or give notice of them, in the Canada Gazette and he or she may also do so in any other manner that he or she considers appropriate.

Section.MarginalNote: Studies, etc. — management or control of obstruction

Subsection.Text: If the Minister considers that doing so is necessary to ensure the free passage of fish or the protection of fish or fish habitat, the owner or person who has the charge, management or control of an obstruction or any other thing that is detrimental to fish or fish habitat shall, on the Minister’s request and within the period specified by him or her, conduct studies, analyses, samplings and evaluations, and provide the Minister with any document and other information relating to them, to the obstruction or thing or to the fish or fish habitat that is or is likely to be affected by the obstruction or thing.

If the Minister considers that doing so is necessary to ensure the free passage of fish or the protection of fish or fish habitat, the owner or person who has the charge, management or control of an obstruction or any other thing that is detrimental to fish or fish habitat shall, on the Minister’s order, within the period specified by him or her and in accordance with any of his or her specifications,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: remove the obstruction or thing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: construct a fishway;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: implement a system of catching fish before the obstruction or thing, transporting them beyond it and releasing them back into the water;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: install a fish stop or a diverter;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: install a fish guard, a screen, a covering, netting or any other device to prevent the passage of fish into any water intake, ditch, channel or canal;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: maintain the flow of water necessary to permit the free passage of fish; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: maintain at all times the characteristics of the water and the water flow downstream of the obstruction or thing that are sufficient for the conservation and protection of the fish and fish habitat.

Subsection.Text: On the Minister’s order, the owner or person referred to in subsection (2) shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make any provision that the Minister considers necessary for the free passage of fish or the protection of fish or fish habitat during the carrying on of any activity mentioned in that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: operate and maintain anything referred to in that subsection in a good and effective condition and in accordance with any specifications of the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: modify or repair it in accordance with any specifications of the Minister.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Obstruction of free passage of fish
Subsection.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: damage or obstruct any fishway constructed or used to enable fish to pass over or around any obstruction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: damage or obstruct any fishway, fish stop or diverter constructed or installed on the Minister’s order;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: stop, impede or hinder fish from entering or passing through any fishway, or stop, impede or hinder fish from surmounting any obstruction or leap;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: damage, remove or authorize the removal of any fish guard, screen, covering, netting or other device installed on the Minister’s order; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: fish in any manner within 23 m downstream from the lower entrance to any fishway, obstruction or leap.

Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph (4)(d), a person may remove or authorize the removal of any fish guard, screen, covering, netting or other device installed on the Minister’s order if the removal is required for modification, repair or maintenance.
Orders made under this section are not statutory instruments for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.
The Minister may make regulations respecting the flow of water that is to be maintained to ensure the free passage of fish or the protection of fish or fish habitat.
No person shall carry on any work, undertaking or activity, other than fishing, that results in the death of fish.
A person may carry on a work, undertaking or activity without contravening subsection (1) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is a prescribed work, undertaking or activity or belongs to a prescribed class of works, undertakings or activities, as the case may be, or is carried on in or around prescribed Canadian fisheries waters, and the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with the prescribed conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the carrying on of the work, undertaking or activity is authorized by the Minister and the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with the conditions established by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the carrying on of the work, undertaking or activity is authorized by a prescribed person or prescribed entity and the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with the conditions set out in the authorization;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the death results from the doing of anything that is authorized, permitted or required under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with the regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with a permit issued under subsection 35.1(3), in the case of a work, undertaking or activity that is part of a designated project and that is designated by the Minister under subsection 35.1(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is a prescribed work, undertaking or activity under paragraph 35.2(10)(a) or belongs to a prescribed class of works, undertakings or activities under that paragraph, as the case may be, and carried on in an ecologically significant area in accordance with an authorization issued under subsection 35.2(7).

Subsection.Text: The prescribed person or prescribed entity referred to in paragraph (2)(c) may, in addition to the prescribed classes of conditions impose, subject to the regulations, any other conditions that they consider appropriate in the circumstances.
The Minister may, for the purposes of paragraph (2)(a) and subject to paragraph 43(1)(i.1), make regulations prescribing anything that is authorized to be prescribed.
The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization issued under paragraph (2)(b).
A prescribed person or prescribed entity referred to in paragraph (2)(c) may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization issued under that paragraph.
Subsection 35(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat

Subsection.Text: No person shall carry on any work, undertaking or activity that results in the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat.
Paragraph 35(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is a prescribed work, undertaking or activity or belongs to a prescribed class of works, undertakings or activities, as the case may be, or is carried on in or around prescribed Canadian fisheries waters, and the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with the prescribed conditions;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 35(2)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the carrying on of the work, undertaking or activity is authorized by a prescribed person or prescribed entity and the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with the conditions set out in the authorization;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction results from the doing of anything that is authorized, permitted or required under this Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 35(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is carried on in accordance with a permit issued under subsection 35.1(3), in the case of a work, undertaking or activity that is part of a designated project and that is designated by the Minister under subsection 35.1(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the work, undertaking or activity is a prescribed work, undertaking or activity under paragraph 35.2(10)(a) or belongs to a prescribed class of works, undertakings or activities under that paragraph, as the case may be, and is carried on in an ecologically significant area in accordance with an authorization issued under subsection 35.2(7).

Subsection.Text: Subsections 35(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The prescribed person or prescribed entity referred to in paragraph (2)(c) may, in addition to the prescribed classes of conditions impose, subject to the regulations, any other conditions that they consider appropriate in the circumstances.
The Minister may, for the purposes of paragraph (2)(a) and subject to paragraph 43(1)(i.1), make regulations prescribing anything that is authorized to be prescribed.
The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization issued under paragraph (2)(b).
A prescribed person or prescribed entity referred to in paragraph (2)(c) may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization issued under that paragraph.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 35:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may designate, as a work, undertaking or activity that is associated with a designated project, any work, undertaking or activity that the Minister considers likely to result in the death of fish or the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Work, undertaking or activity designated by Minister
Subsection.Text: The Minister shall designate any work, undertaking or activity that is part of a designated project and that the Minister considers likely to result in the death of fish or the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat.
The Minister may issue a permit to carry on any work, undertaking or activity designated under subsection (2) and attach any conditions to it.
No person shall carry on any work, undertaking or activity that is designated under subsection (2) except in accordance with a permit issued under subsection (3).
The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel a permit issued under subsection (3).

No person shall carry on a work, undertaking or activity prescribed under paragraph (10)(a) or that belongs to a prescribed class under that paragraph, in an ecologically significant area except in accordance with an authorization issued under subsection (7).
The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations designating ecologically significant areas.

Any person who proposes to carry on a work, undertaking or activity referred to in subsection (1) in an ecologically significant area shall provide the Minister with any document and other information that is required by regulation in respect of the prescribed work, undertaking or activity, or the water, place, fish or fish habitat that is likely to be affected by the prescribed work, undertaking or activity.
Regulations made for the purpose of subsection (3) do not prevent the Minister from requesting additional information that he or she considers necessary in the circumstances.
Every person who is required to provide any additional information must provide it within the time and in the manner that the Minister specifies.
The Minister may, on request in writing from any person who is required to provide any additional information, extend the specified time.

If the Minister is satisfied, after having reviewed any document and other information provided under subsection (3) or (4), that avoidance and mitigation measures may be implemented to achieve the prescribed objectives for the conservation and protection of fish and fish habitat, he or she may authorize, subject to the regulations made under subsection (10), the carrying on of the work, undertaking or activity referred to in subsection (1) in an ecologically significant area, on any conditions that he or she considers appropriate.
The Minister may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization issued under subsection (7).

The Minister shall, as soon as feasible, prepare a fish habitat restoration plan for an ecologically significant area, if he or she is of the opinion that fish habitat restoration in that ecologically significant area is required in order to meet any prescribed objectives for the conservation and protection of fish and fish habitat.
The Governor in Council may, on the Minister’s recommendation, make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing works, undertakings or activities or classes of works, undertakings or activities, for the purposes of this section;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting any document or other information that is required to be provided under subsection (3), including the manner in which and the time within which it is to be provided;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the objectives for the conservation and protection of fish and fish habitat in an ecologically significant area;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing works, undertakings or activities or classes of works, undertakings or activities that the Minister shall not authorize under paragraphs 34.4(2)(b) and 35(2)(b) to be carried on in an ecologically significant area;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing conditions under which and requirements subject to which the Minister may issue an authorization under subsection (7);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner and circumstances relating to the amendment, suspension or cancellation of an authorization referred to in subsection (7); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the process by which a person may request an amendment, suspension or cancellation of an authorization under subsection (7).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 37(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Minister may require plans and specifications

Subsection.Text: If a person carries on or proposes to carry on any work, undertaking or activity that results or is likely to result in the death of fish, in the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat or in the deposit of a deleterious substance in water frequented by fish or in any place under any conditions where that deleterious substance or any other deleterious substance that results from the deposit of that deleterious substance may enter any such waters, the person shall, on the request of the Minister — or without request in the manner and circumstances prescribed by regulations made under paragraph (3)(a) — provide him or her with any documents — plans, specifications, studies, procedures, schedules, analyses, samples, evaluations — and any other information relating to the work, undertaking or activity, or to the water, place, fish or fish habitat that is or is likely to be affected by the work, undertaking or activity, that will enable the Minister to determine

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the work, undertaking or activity results or is likely to result in the death of fish that constitutes or would constitute an offence under subsection 40(1) and what measures, if any, would prevent that death or mitigate the extent of death;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: whether the work, undertaking or activity results or is likely to result in the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat that constitutes or would constitute an offence under subsection 40(1) and what measures, if any, would prevent that result or mitigate its effects; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 37(1)(b) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: si l’ouvrage, l’entreprise ou l’activité entraîne ou entraînera vraisemblablement l’immersion ou le rejet d’une substance en contravention avec l’article 36 et quelles sont les mesures éventuelles à prendre pour prévenir ou atténuer les dommages qui en découlent.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 37(1.1) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 37(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If, after reviewing any document and other information provided under subsection (1) and affording the persons who provided it a reasonable opportunity to make representations, the Minister is of the opinion that an offence under subsection 40(1) or (2) is being or is likely to be committed, he or she may, by order, subject to regulations made under paragraph (3)(b),
Paragraph 37(2)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: require any modifications or additions to the work, undertaking or activity or any modifications to any plans, specifications, procedures or schedules relating to it that the Minister considers necessary in the circumstances, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 37(2) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The Minister may personally direct the closing of the work or undertaking or the ending of the activity for any period that he or she considers necessary in the circumstances.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 37(3)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the manner and circumstances in which any document or other information is to be provided to the Minister without request under subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the manner and circumstances in which the Minister may make orders under subsection (2) and the terms of the orders.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 37(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Minister proposes to make an order under subsection (2), he or she shall offer to consult with the governments of any provinces that he or she considers to be interested in the proposed order and with any departments or agencies of the Government of Canada that he or she considers appropriate.

Nothing in subsection (4) prevents the Minister from making an interim order under subsection (2) without the offer of consultation referred to in subsection (4) if he or she considers that immediate action is necessary.
Orders made under this section are not statutory instruments for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.
Subsection 38(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may designate persons or classes of persons as inspectors for the purposes of the administration and enforcement of this Act and may limit in any manner he or she considers appropriate the powers that an inspector may exercise under this Act.
Subparagraph 38(3)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the death of fish,

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: the harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 38(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to notify — serious harm to fish
Subsection.Text: Every person shall without delay notify an inspector, a fishery officer, a fishery guardian or an authority prescribed by the regulations of an occurrence that results in serious harm to fish that are part of a commercial, recreational or Indigenous fishery, or to fish that support such a fishery, that is not authorized under this Act, or of a serious and imminent danger of such an occurrence, if the person at any material time
The portion of subsection 38(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to notify — death of fish
Subsection.Text: Every person shall without delay notify an inspector, a fishery officer, a fishery guardian or an authority prescribed by the regulations of the death of fish that is not authorized under this Act, or of a serious and imminent danger of such occurrence, if the person at any material time
Paragraphs 38(4)(a) and (b) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: la personne qui est responsable, à titre de propriétaire ou autrement, de l’ouvrage, de l’entreprise ou de l’activité à l’origine de l’évènement effectif ou fort probable et imminent;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: celle qui est à l’origine de l’évènement effectif ou fort probable et imminent, ou y contribue.

Subsection.Text: Section 38 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to notify — harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat
Subsection.Text: Every person shall without delay notify an inspector, a fishery officer, a fishery guardian or an authority prescribed by the regulations of a harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat that is not authorized under this Act, or of a serious and imminent danger of such an occurrence, if the person at any material time

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: owns or has the charge, management or control of the work, undertaking or activity that resulted in the occurrence or the danger of the occurrence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: causes or contributes to the occurrence or the danger of the occurrence.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 38(5) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If there occurs a deposit of a deleterious substance in water frequented by fish that is not authorized under this Act, or if there is a serious and imminent danger of such an occurrence, and detriment to fish habitat or fish or to the use by humans of fish results or may reasonably be expected to result from the occurrence, then every person shall without delay notify an inspector, a fishery officer, a fishery guardian or an authority prescribed by the regulations if the person at any material time
Subsection 38(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Any person described in paragraph (4)(a) or (b), (4.1)(a) or (b) or (5)(a) or (b) shall, as soon as feasible, take all reasonable measures consistent with public safety and with the conservation and protection of fish and fish habitat to prevent the occurrence or to counteract, mitigate or remedy any adverse effects that result from the occurrence or might reasonably be expected to result from it.
Subsection 38(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

As soon as feasible after the occurrence or after learning of the danger of the occurrence, the person shall provide an inspector, a fishery officer, a fishery guardian or an authority prescribed by the regulations with a written report on the occurrence or danger of the occurrence.
Subsection 38(7.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an inspector or fishery officer, whether or not they have been notified under subsection (4), (4.1) or (5) or provided with a report under subsection (7), is satisfied on reasonable grounds that immediate action is necessary in order to take any measures referred to in subsection (6), the inspector or officer may, subject to subsection (7.2), take any of those measures at the expense of any person described in paragraph (4)(a) or (b), (4.1)(a) or (b) or (5)(a) or (b) or direct that person to take the measures at their expense.
Paragraph 38(9)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the authority for the purposes of subsection (4), (4.1) or (5), the manner in which the notification under those subsections is to be made, the information to be contained in the notification and the circumstances in which no notification need be made;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 38(11) to (13) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 39:
Every power that may be exercised in Canada by an inspector under this Act may be exercised anywhere this Act applies.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 40(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person who contravenes subsection 34.4(1) or 35(1) is guilty of an offence and liable
Paragraphs 40(3)(a) and (a.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in carrying on a work, undertaking or activity, fails to comply with a prescribed condition referred to in paragraph 34.4(2)(a) or 35(2)(a), with a condition set out in an authorization or a permit, as the case may be, issued under paragraph 34.4(2)(b) or (c) or 35(2)(b) or (c) or subsection 35.1(3) or 35.2(7), or with a condition imposed by regulations made under subsection 36(5) or (5.2),

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: fails to provide any document or other information as requested by the Minister under subsection 37(1) within a reasonable time after the request is made,

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: fails to provide any document or other information required to be provided under subsection 35.2(3) within the prescribed period,

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: fails to provide any additional information that is required to be provided under subsection 35.2(4) within the time and in the manner that the Minister specifies,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 40(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: fails to provide notification that he or she is required to provide under subsection 38 (4), (4.1) or (5),

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 40(3)(d) of the Act before subparagraph (iii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: carries on any work, undertaking or activity described in subsection 37(1)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: otherwise than in accordance with any document and other information relating to the work, undertaking or activity that they provide to the Minister under that subsection,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: otherwise than in accordance with any such document and other information as required to be modified by any order of the Minister under paragraph 37(2)(a), or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: carries on any work, undertaking or activity described in subsection 35.2(3) otherwise than in accordance with any document and other information relating to the work, undertaking or activity that they provide to the Minister under that subsection or subsection 35.2(4),

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(3) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (g) and by replacing paragraph (h) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with a request of the Minister made under subsection 34.3(1), an order of the Minister made under subsection 34.3(2) or (3) or a provision of a regulation made under subsection 34.3(7),

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with all or any part of a direction of a fishery officer or fishery guardian with respect to the application of any regulations made under paragraph 43(1)(o),

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: carries on any work, undertaking or activity that is a part of a designated project in contravention of subsection 35.1(4), or

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: carries on, in contravention of subsection 35.2(1), any work, undertaking or activity prescribed under paragraph 35.2(10)(a),

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 40(5) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
For the purpose of any proceedings for an offence under any of subsections (1), (2) and (3),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the death of fish takes place whether or not any act or omission resulting in the death is intentional;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: a harmful alteration, disruption or destruction of fish habitat takes place whether or not an act or omission resulting in the alteration, disruption or destruction is intentional;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: a deposit as defined in subsection 34(1) takes place whether or not any act or omission resulting in the deposit is intentional; and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 42:
The following definitions apply in this section and sections 42.02 to 42.04.

Definition.Text: conservation project means a work, undertaking or activity that is carried on by a proponent for the purpose of creating, restoring or enhancing fish habitat within a service area in order to acquire habitat credits. (projet de conservation)

fish habitat bank means an area of a fish habitat that has been created, restored or enhanced by the carrying on of one or more conservation projects within a service area and in respect of which area the Minister has certified any habitat credit under paragraph 42.02(1)(b). (réserve d’habitats)
habitat credit means a unit of measure that is agreed to between any proponent and the Minister under section 42.02 that quantifies the benefits of a conservation project. (crédit d’habitat)
proponent means a person who proposes the carrying on of a conservation project and any other work, undertaking or activity within a proposed service area. (promoteur)

service area means the geographical area that encompasses a fish habitat bank and one or more conservation projects and within which area a proponent carries on a work, undertaking or activity. (zone de service)

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of sections 42.01 to 42.04, the Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish a system for the creation, allocation and management of a proponent’s habitat credits in relation to a conservation project; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: issue a certificate to the proponent respecting the validity of any habitat credit acquired from the carrying on of a conservation project.

Subsection.Text: In exercising the powers referred to in subsection (1), the Minister may enter into arrangements with any proponent.
An arrangement referred to in subsection (2) shall include, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any document and other information that describes the proposed fish habitat bank, conservation project and service area;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a written confirmation that the Department of Fisheries and Oceans and anyone authorized to act on the Department’s behalf is authorized to access the site of the conservation project for the term of the arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the administration, management and general operation of the arrangement by the parties, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a procedure for proposing a conservation project and an approval process,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a habitat credit certification process,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a process for habitat credit evaluation and any re-evaluation that may be required by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: habitat credit accounting procedures respecting the habitat credit ledger,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: progress reports on the conservation project, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: any other relevant matters respecting the administration of the arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: reports on the performance of the arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the form and manner in which the arrangement may be amended;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the date on which the arrangement comes into force; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the signatures of the parties.

Section.MarginalNote: Use of habitat credit within service area
Section.Text: A proponent may only use their certified habitat credits in respect of a fish habitat bank within a service area to offset the adverse effects on fish or fish habitat from the carrying on of a work, undertaking or activity authorized or permitted to be carried on in that service area.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the establishment of a system for the creation, allocation and management of habitat credits referred to in paragraph 42.02(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the issuance of a certificate of validity of any habitat credit referred to in paragraph 42.02(1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting an arrangement with any proponent.

Section.Text: Subsection 42.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall, as soon as feasible after the end of each fiscal year, prepare and cause to be laid before each house of Parliament a report on the administration and enforcement of the provisions of this Act relating to fish and fish habitat protection and pollution prevention for that year.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 42.1:
The Minister shall establish a public registry for the purpose of facilitating access to records relating to matters under any of sections 34 to 42.1.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall publish the following records in the registry:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any agreements referred to in section 4.1 that are entered into by him or her and that establish the circumstances and manner referred to in paragraph 4.1(2)(h);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any standards and codes of practice established by the Minister under section 34.2;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any orders made by the Minister under sections 34.3 and 37;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any authorizations given under paragraphs 34.4(2)(b) and (c) and 35(2)(b) and (c) and subsection 35.2(7);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any permits issued by him or her under section 35.1; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any fish habitat restoration plan prepared under subsection 35.2(9).

Subsection.Text: The Minister may publish in the registry, other records that he or she considers appropriate for the purpose set out in section 42.2, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any agreements referred to in section 4.1 and subsection 4.4(3);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any arrangements referred to in subsection 4.4(3) and section 42.02;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any proposed regulations;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any reports submitted under any regulations made under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any guidelines; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any policy.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on the request of any other minister designated under section 43.2, publish any records in the registry that the designated minister considers appropriate for the purpose set out in section 42.2.
Despite subsections (1) to (3), the registry shall contain only records or any part of those records

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that are publicly available; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the Minister determines would be disclosed to the public in accordance with the Access to Information Act if a request were made in respect of that record under that Act, including any record that would be disclosed in the public interest under subsection 20(6) of that Act.

Section.Text: The Minister may determine the form of the registry, how it is to be kept and how access to it is to be provided.

Section.MarginalNote: Protection from civil proceeding or prosecution
Section.Text: Despite any other Act of Parliament, civil or criminal proceedings shall not be brought against Her Majesty in right of Canada, the Minister and any person acting on behalf of or under the direction of the Minister for the full or partial disclosure of any record through the registry made in good faith or for any consequences of its disclosure.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 43(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the proper management and control of the seacoast and inland fisheries, including for social, economic or cultural purposes;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 43(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the rebuilding of fish stocks;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the restoration of fish habitat;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 43(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances when the holder of a licence or the operator named in the licence is required to personally carry on the activity authorized by the licence and the exceptions to that requirement;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 43(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the issuance, suspension and cancellation of licences and leases, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the lease or licence holder or the applicant for that lease or licence is a party to an agreement in respect of the lease or licence that contravenes any provision of this Act or of the regulations, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the applicant for the lease or licence is a corporation;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 43(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (g.01)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the use and control of the rights and privileges under a lease or licence issued under this Act, including the prohibition on the transfer of the use and control of those rights and privileges except under prescribed conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (g.02)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a licence issued to an organization, respecting the designation of persons who may fish and the fishing vessels that may be used under the licence and any other matter relating to designations, including the method of designation and who may designate those persons and vessels;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 43(1)(i) to (i.4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the conservation and protection of fish habitat;

Paragraph.Label: (i.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of paragraphs 34.4(2)(a) and 35(2)(a), prescribing anything that is authorized to be prescribed under those paragraphs;

Paragraph.Label: (i.11)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the process relating to the issuance of the authorizations referred to in paragraph 34.4(2)(b) or (c) or 35(2)(b) or (c) or the permits referred to in subsection 35.1(3);

Paragraph.Label: (i.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the documents or other information that are to be provided for the obtaining of the authorizations referred to in paragraph 34.4(2)(b) or (c) or 35(2)(b) or (c) or the permits referred to in subsection 35.1(3), or for the amendment, suspension or cancellation of those authorizations or permits;

Paragraph.Label: (i.21)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing persons or entities who may authorize the carrying on of works, undertakings or activities under paragraphs 34.4(2)(c) and 35(2)(c) and respecting the requirements that these persons or entities may be subject to;

Paragraph.Label: (i.3)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the conditions and requirements under which a person or entity referred to in paragraph 34.4(2)(c) or 35(2)(c) may issue the authorization;

Paragraph.Label: (i.31)
Paragraph.Text: with respect to an authorization issued under 34.4(2)(c) or 35(2)(c), prescribing the classes of conditions that the prescribed person or entity

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shall include in the authorization,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: may include in it, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: shall not include in it;

Paragraph.Label: (i.4)
Paragraph.Text: respecting time limits for issuing authorizations referred to in paragraph 34.4(2)(b) or (c) or 35(2)(b) or (c) or for issuing permits under subsection 35.1(3), or for refusing to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (i.5)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of the definition designated project in subsection 34(1), designating projects or classes of projects that are likely to affect fish or fish habitat;

Paragraph.Label: (i.6)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the time, manner and circumstances in which the Minister may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization referred to in subsection 34.4(5) or 35(5), or a permit referred to in subsection 35.1(5), as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (i.7)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the time, manner and circumstances in which the prescribed person or prescribed entity may amend, suspend or cancel an authorization referred to in subsection 34.4(6) or 35(6);

Paragraph.Label: (i.8)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the process by which a person may request an amendment, suspension or cancellation of an authorization under subsection 34.4(5) or (6) or 35(5) or (6), or a permit under subsection 35.1(5), as the case may be;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 43(1)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the import or export of fish;

Paragraph.Label: (j.1)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances in which the Indigenous knowledge of the Indigenous peoples of Canada that is provided to the Minister under this Act in confidence may be disclosed without written consent;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 43(1)(m) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: if a close time, fishing quota or limit on the size or weight of fish or fishing gear or equipment has been fixed in respect of an area under the regulations, authorizing persons referred to in paragraph (l) to vary the close time, fishing quota or limit or fishing gear or equipment in respect of that area or any portion of that area;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 43(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (n):

Paragraph.Label: (n.1)
Paragraph.Text: defining aquatic invasive species for the purposes of the regulations;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 43(1)(o) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the management and control of aquatic invasive species, including regulations

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the prevention of the introduction and spread of those species,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 43(1)(o) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by replacing subparagraph (v) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the treatment and destruction of members of those species,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the eradication of those species in a specified geographical area,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the powers of a fishery officer and fishery guardian to manage and control those species,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: authorizing a fishery officer and fishery guardian to exercise their powers with respect to a species that the officer or guardian, as the case may be, has reasonable grounds to believe is an aquatic invasive species, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: requiring any person to keep any record, book or other document containing any information relevant to the control of those species, and respecting where, how and how long it is to be kept; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 43(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Regulations made under paragraph (1)(i.5) may include as a criterion for designation the fact that a decision has been made under an Act of Parliament to subject the project to an impact assessment.
Subsection 43(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Regulations exempting certain Canadian fisheries waters
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations exempting any Canadian fisheries waters from the application of sections 34.3, 34.4 and 35 and subsections 38(4) and (4.1).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 43.2:
